title: Logic Diagrams and Gate Implementation
summary: Covers logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, NXOR), their truth tables, symbols, and the concept of minimal sets of generators.
content_points: Logic gates implement Boole functions; truth tables describe each gate's input-output behaviour.
Elementary gates: AND, OR, NOT. Derived: NAND, NOR, XOR, NXOR.
A set of generators can implement any Boole function; minimal sets: {NAND} or {NOR} (single gate type suffices).
Associative gates can be extended to more than 2 inputs.
learning_objectives: Draw truth tables for AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, NXOR
Identify minimal sets of gate generators
Implement simple Boole functions using logic diagrams
